title: salix schwerinii subsp. yezoensis
intro: Salix schwerinii subsp. yezoensis, also known as Japanese willow, is a subspecies of the Salix schwerinii plant, which is a type of willow tree native to Japan. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salix schwerinii subsp. yezoensis include reducing fever, alleviating headaches and body aches, and treating various skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
